name: Abdul Sattar Edhi International Foundation
mission: PROVIDE SHELTER,CLOTHING,FOOD TO POOR AND THE NEEDY PEOPLE AROUND THE WORLD.
theoryOfChange: The website describes service delivery aimed at easing suffering through healthcare, education, and social welfare; examples include providing free treatment for those who cannot afford it, operating schools to break barriers to education, and welfare centers extending services to people at their door-steps.
scoreSummary: Abdul Sattar Edhi International Foundation Inc shows strong alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: Found 'fidya' on https://edhi.org/ (https://edhi.org/); Website donation methods mention zakat

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 120 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

keyConcerns.headline: No outcome measurement or metrics tracking

components.evidence: Accepts zakat (+2); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Asnaf: fuqara (+5); Operates in Muslim-majority regions (+3); Strong humanitarian service (+4)
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.evidence: Niche cause: UNKNOWN (+4); Serves underserved populations (+3)
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 38/50: HIGH Muslim donor fit, religious cultural cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; high program ratio (≥85%) suggests efficient delivery
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish a time-bound plan to deploy excess unrestricted reserves into mission delivery, while keeping a clear operating reserve floor.
components.name: Financial Health

judge_issues.message: The rationale states that the overall score is moderated by a lack of external performance ratings, but Charity Navigator provides a 3-star rating (80%), which should be considered an external performance rating. This suggests the rationale is incomplete.

The impact rationale does not adequately address the significant deduction for excessive working capital (120.5 months), which heavily impacts the impact score. This omission makes the rationale incomplete.
Website shows related EIN (11-3454067), likely parent/subsidiary of 11-3013369
Website citation for 'claims_zakat_eligible' uses homepage URL; deep link preferred
Website citation for 'founded_year' uses homepage URL; deep link preferred
Website citation for 'has_audited_financials' uses homepage URL; deep link preferred
Extremely high working capital (120.5 months) may indicate hoarding or data error

all_citations.claim: The organization provides donation options and is recognized for Zakat-eligible humanitarian work.
The foundation reported total revenue of $5,242,874 and operates programs including ambulance services and highway projects.
The charity maintains a program expense ratio of 95.1%, 120.5 months of working capital, and a fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The foundation earns a 65/100 GMG score due to its outstanding financial efficiency and Zakat-eligible status, though its overall score is moderated by a lack of external performance ratings.
areas_for_improvement: Limited third-party impact evaluations or cost-per-beneficiary data
High accumulation of working capital relative to annual program spending

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows high donor fit for those seeking religious and cultural alignment, as it is verified as Zakat-eligible [1]. Its mission to serve the needy aligns with high-urgency humanitarian causes, supported by a long-standing track record in international relief [1][2].
dimension_explanations.impact: The charity demonstrates a direct service model with a significant focus on infrastructure, including a High Ways Project every 25 kilometers and a dedicated ambulance service [2]. While it maintains a high program expense ratio of 95.1%, specific data regarding the cost per beneficiary is currently unavailable [2][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Abdul Sattar Edhi International Foundation Inc provides critical humanitarian relief through a highly efficient financial model that directs nearly all funds to direct services.
strengths: Exceptional program expense ratio of 95.1%, indicating high financial efficiency
Zero-cost fundraising model with $0.00 spent per $1 raised
Extensive liquid assets with 120.5 months of working capital
baseline_narrative.summary: The organization is dedicated to providing shelter, clothing, and food to impoverished populations globally while maintaining a robust ambulance and educational network [2]. With a total revenue of $5,242,874, the foundation operates with a program expense ratio of 95.1%, ensuring that the vast majority of contributions directly support its mission [2][3].

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The foundation earns a 65/100 GMG score due to its outstanding financial efficiency and Zakat-eligible status, though its overall score is moderated by a lack of external performance ratings.

areas_for_improvement.context: The organization holds <cite id="9">over 10 years of operating reserves</cite>, which may be viewed as inefficient for a Zakat-collecting entity that should prioritize prompt distribution.
While financial efficiency is high, there is a <cite id="11">lack of consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to assess the specific cost-effectiveness of individual programs.

bbb_assessment.summary: The organization provides audited financial statements but is not currently evaluated by the BBB Wise Giving Alliance.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The foundation continues to make <cite id="7">multi-million dollar grants</cite> to its primary implementing partners in Pakistan.
risk_factors: <cite id="9">Excessive liquidity</cite> may signal a slow rate of aid distribution relative to needs.
case_against.summary: The primary concern is the <cite id="9">accumulation of 120+ months of reserves</cite>, which is unusual for a humanitarian charity.

confidence.notes: Strong financial transparency but limited impact measurement data.

alignment.explanation: Excellent fit for Muslim donors as it <cite id="14">accepts Zakat</cite> and serves the <cite id="1">fuqara and masakin</cite> in Muslim-majority regions.

credibility.explanation: The foundation is highly credible due to its <cite id="12">Silver Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite> and a <cite id="10">fully independent board</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the organization should <cite id="13">expand its board beyond 4 members</cite> to ensure more robust oversight.

impact.explanation: Impact is driven by a <cite id="4">100.0% program ratio</cite> and direct service delivery in <cite id="2">healthcare and emergency response</cite>.
impact.improvement: The foundation should <cite id="11">publish a time-bound plan to deploy excess reserves</cite> into active mission delivery.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade C - Verified outcomes with limited cost-per-beneficiary data

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Funds the primary operational entity in Pakistan to execute direct services.

rich_narrative.headline: The Abdul Sattar Edhi International Foundation Inc provides critical humanitarian relief through a highly efficient financial model that directs nearly all funds to direct services.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value a legendary humanitarian legacy and want 95.1% of their contribution to reach the field without any deductions for fundraising or marketing.
donor_motivations: Want to support the specific legacy of Abdul Sattar Edhi
Focus on direct humanitarian aid in Pakistan
Seeking a Zakat-eligible vehicle with massive infrastructure
giving_considerations: The organization holds very high cash reserves
Impact reporting is focused on service volume rather than academic RCTs
Governance is centralized within a small board
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking high-growth startups or those who prefer charities with low cash reserves and immediate 'spend-down' policies.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: While the foundation has a verified track record of service delivery, it lacks granular cost-per-beneficiary data and independent impact evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The foundation operates on a direct-service model where providing immediate essentials (food, medicine, shelter) and emergency transport (ambulances) directly reduces mortality and suffering among the destitute.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the massive scale of the Edhi network translates into measurable improvements in life expectancy and poverty reduction.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While the foundation has high reserves, the scale of poverty in Pakistan allows for significant expansion of its ambulance and hospital networks.
strategic_priorities: Maintenance of the world's largest voluntary ambulance fleet
Expansion of orphanage and shelter services

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by a <cite id="5">zero-cost fundraising model</cite> and a <cite id="4">95.1% program expense ratio</cite>.
similar_orgs: Helping Hand for Relief and Development

strengths.detail: The organization directs a <cite id="4">program expense ratio of 95.1%</cite> toward its mission, far exceeding industry benchmarks for humanitarian aid.
The foundation spends <cite id="5">$0.00 to raise every $1</cite>, ensuring that donor contributions are not eroded by marketing or solicitation costs.
With a history <cite id="8">dating back to 1949</cite>, the foundation is one of the most trusted names in the Muslim world for direct service delivery.

strengths_deep_dive: The foundation's strength lies in its massive operational scale and liquidity. It currently maintains <cite id="9">120.5 months of working capital</cite> in working capital, providing an extraordinary level of financial stability. This allows the organization to respond to crises without immediate fundraising pressure. Furthermore, the board maintains <cite id="10">95.1% independent members</cite>, ensuring that governance remains focused on the founder's original mission of serving the 'poor and the needy people around the world' through <cite id="1">hospitals, homes, and children's services</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: The Abdul Sattar Edhi International Foundation Inc. is the international arm of one of the world's most storied humanitarian organizations, dedicated to <cite id="1">providing shelter, clothing, and food</cite> to the poor and needy. founded in 1949, the organization has built a massive infrastructure that includes <cite id="2">ambulance services, educational programs, and orphanage centers</cite> primarily serving Pakistan. The foundation operates with a unique financial philosophy that prioritizes direct aid above all else, reporting <cite id="3">total revenue of $5,752,945</cite> in its most recent filing. Remarkably, the organization maintains a <cite id="4">program expense ratio of 95.1%</cite>, meaning every dollar of functional expense is categorized toward its mission-driven activities. This efficiency is further highlighted by a <cite id="5">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, as the foundation does not employ traditional aggressive fundraising tactics, relying instead on its long-standing reputation. While the organization holds significant <cite id="6">net assets of $27,402,805</cite>, it continues to facilitate large-scale transfers to its implementing partners, including <cite id="7">over $5.1 million in domestic grants</cite> to the A S Edhi Foundation to sustain its frontline operations.
